note: Deck-style segment battles (modeled on 'Competitive Landscape - December 2024.pptx'). Ratings: good | soso | bad, from Cardo's perspective, based on agent research + review consensus as of July 2026. Dimension sub-criteria mirror the internal deck.

dimensions_legend.Ease of Use: Wheel, cradle, mount, voice commands, screen, OTA
dimensions_legend.Intercom: Range, # riders, mesh, auto-reconnect, WB sound
dimensions_legend.Quality: Build quality, service, IP65/IP67, water resistance
dimensions_legend.Range: Max intercom range; cellular/Wave = effectively unlimited
dimensions_legend.Group size: Max riders in one intercom group
dimensions_legend.Battery life: Rated talk time hours per charge
dimensions_legend.Voice / AI: Voice commands and AI-assistant capability (excludes AI noise-cancellation)
dimensions_legend.Software & OTA: Companion app and over-the-air feature updates

battles.title: SPIRIT vs competition
battles.exec_summary: Spirit still anchors the range on brand trust and OTA support, but at this price point ASMAX now sells MESH with an offline AI assistant and a published waterproof rating — a spec sheet Spirit cannot match. Sena raised 5R LITE's price to $109 in July 2026, so it now costs about the same as Spirit rather than undercutting it — the entry tier is defended by distribution and brand, not by product.

matchups.blurb: Sena's lowest-profile entry unit; basic 2-rider Bluetooth, water-resistant. Repriced from $99 to $109 in July 2026 (store-us.sena.com), now roughly at parity with Spirit.
Mesh + BT 5.4 dual-chip, offline 'Hi Max' AI voice, IPX7, FOTA — flagship-class spec at the cheapest price in the market. Amazon street price dropped from $89.99 to $83.99 in July 2026.
Bluetooth entry model sold via SEA dealers; specs opaque, not on the US store.

battles.title: SPIRIT HD vs competition
battles.exec_summary: Spirit HD's 40mm HD speakers and sound profiles carry it against Sena's aging 5-series, but Sena discounts aggressively ($135 for the 5S with LCD screen) and ASMAX Z1 sells mesh at half the price. HD audio is the story; price is the exposure.

matchups.blurb: Entry unit with integrated LCD screen and jog dial; currently 20% off on Sena's store.
Slim 3-button 4-rider Bluetooth unit, reviewed as strong value (Ultimate Motorcycling).
Mid-positioned mesh unit with 40mm HiFi speakers and claimed 160 km/h voice clarity, sold at entry price.
cardo_advantage: Brand trust, dealer network, proven firmware
competitor_advantage: MESH at half the price (10 riders)

battles.title: FREECOM 2X vs competition
battles.exec_summary: Freecom 2x sells premium 2-rider Bluetooth with JBL sound. Sena's Spider RT1/ST1 was repriced from $199 to $239 in July 2026 and is now marked out of stock on Sena's own store (possibly being wound down ahead of the Spider X Slim successor) — Freecom 2x now actually undercuts it at $210 street, though Spider still offers 24-rider MESH vs Freecom's 2-rider BT. ASMAX Future 1 still brings mesh + AI at $155. A 2-rider BT unit above $200 is structurally hard to defend in 2026; JBL audio and Live Intercom UX are the remaining pillars.

matchups.price: $239 (out of stock on Sena's own store)
matchups.blurb: Affordable Mesh 2.0-only unit (24 riders, 8 km multi-hop). Repriced from $199 to $239 in July 2026, and all variants now show out of stock on store-us.sena.com — a likely sign the line is being sunset ahead of the Spider X Slim (Mesh 3.0) successor announced at EICMA 2025.
cardo_advantage: $29 cheaper at street now that Spider RT1/ST1 is repriced to $239

matchups.blurb: 2025 EICMA model: mesh, neural-network noise filtering, AI voice, 1350 mAh, IPX7 — 'high-tech at an affordable price' per Asian reviewers.
cardo_advantage: Western retail availability (Future 1 not on US store yet)

battles.title: FREECOM 4X vs competition
battles.exec_summary: The 4-rider Freecom 4x holds the 'premium Bluetooth' niche on JBL sound and polish; its $270 street price now actually undercuts Sena's mesh-capable 50R (confirmed $375 MSRP / $337.50 street), though it still doubles ASMAX's F1 Pro (~$205, mesh + Bongiovi audio). The 2025 MSRP hike ($269.95 → $314.95) made this segment position visibly worse even as the 50R comparison eases.

matchups.blurb: Low-profile Mesh 2.0 unit with Harman Kardon audio — now confirmed pricier than Freecom 4x, but still brings mesh where Cardo doesn't at this tier.
Octa-core mesh unit with Bongiovi 'Grammy-level' tuning, 14h intercom / 21h music, AI voice — not yet on the US store.

battles.title: PACKTALK NEO vs competition
battles.exec_summary: Neo delivers full DMC Gen2 (31 riders post Mesh-Boost) at $80 under the Edge — but Reso's Pilot Neo undercuts it by ~$90 with IP67, 24h battery and 3-yr warranty, and Sena's 50S now drops to $262 street (Amazon storefront), nearly $100 under the Neo. Neo wins on mesh reliability and ecosystem; it loses the pure value math even more decisively than before. This is the deck's 2024 'Neo vs Pilot Pro' story repeating one tier down. Sena's June 2026 Spider X Slim adds a fifth mid-mesh contender: Bose audio and a lifetime warranty at $299, undercutting the Neo by $61 but giving up cross-brand bridging and battery life.

matchups.blurb: Aging Mesh 2.0 mid-tier with Harman Kardon speakers and jog dial; firmware v2.7 drew community backlash; refresh expected as 2026 range lands.
competitor_advantage: Jog dial preferred by some riders

matchups.blurb: Single-channel mesh (15 riders), IP67, 24h talk, 15W fast charge, 3-yr warranty — the sharpest value attack on the Neo.

matchups.price: $269 street (Malaysia dealer; not yet on US store)
matchups.blurb: EICMA 2025 top model: 45mm speakers, 1350 mAh, Bongiovi tuning — first confirmed price is well under the Neo.
competitor_advantage: $90 cheaper than Neo at confirmed SEA pricing

matchups.blurb: New June 2026 mesh-only mid-tier: ultra-slim body with separate battery pack, Sound by Bose 40mm drivers, AI noise cancellation and Sena's lifetime warranty — undercuts the 60-series and directly challenges the Neo's value math.
cardo_advantage: Universal cross-brand BT bridging (Spider X Slim is mesh-only, no BT intercom)
Established DMC reliability record vs brand-new hardware
competitor_advantage: Lifetime warranty vs Cardo's limited coverage
Slimmer, lighter form factor (23.2g main unit)
to_test: Real-world mesh range/reliability (too new for reviewer consensus)

battles.title: PACKTALK PRO vs competition
battles.exec_summary: Cardo still owns the reliability crown — DMC, IP67, crash detection, and nearly every 'best overall' verdict. But the price umbrella is under attack from three directions: Sena 60S EVO ($459) with Bose audio + lifetime warranty, Reso Pilot Pro ($359) matching the safety stack, and ASMAX F1s ($120 with Amazon coupon, down from $140) resetting the price floor even lower. Wave/CloudTalk-style cellular intercom is the marketing capability gap; the 2025 MSRP hikes are the self-inflicted one.

matchups.blurb: May 2026 flagship: Sound by Bose, Mesh 3.0 + Wave cellular intercom, 27h talk, IPX7, limited lifetime warranty. MCN: 'considerably cheaper than Cardo's flagship'.
competitor_advantage: Wave = unlimited range over LTE (headline feature)
to_test: EVO AI noise cancellation at speed (60S version underperformed >80 km/h)

matchups.blurb: Launched July 2, 2026: Sena's first joystick-controlled intercom, replacing the jog dial with glove-friendly buttons, plus magnetic mount with Qi wireless charging, Sound by Bose, Mesh 3.0/Wave, and a limited lifetime warranty — same price as the 60S EVO.
cardo_advantage: Proven Natural Voice + Air Mount UX
competitor_advantage: Joystick control pitched as easier with gloves than Cardo's wheel
to_test: Joystick usability at speed/in gloves vs Cardo's control wheel
Real-world Bose audio verdict once reviewed

matchups.blurb: 2024 flagship now discounted ~20% as EVO lands — price pressure directly on the Edge.
Ex-DJI team: DuoSync hardware+software mesh (30 riders), GNSS crash SOS, 'Hey RESO', GoPro/DJI/Insta360 audio-in-footage, IP67, 3-yr warranty.

The price-floor reset: mesh + offline AI voice + IP67 + FOTA at ~24% of Packtalk Pro's price (down from ~28% before the July 2026 coupon price drop). Range claims don't survive testing, but the value optics damage the whole premium tier.

battles.title: PACKTALK EDGE vs competition
battles.exec_summary: The Edge is the previous-gen DMC flagship, now the smart value pick below the Packtalk Pro: same 2nd-gen Dynamic Mesh, 40mm JBL audio and IP67 build, minus the Pro's IMU crash detection. At $396 street it collides head-on with the Sena 60S, which is also ~$351 discounted post-EVO and counters with Wave cellular intercom and a 24-rider mesh — though as of July 2026 the 60S is showing out of stock on Sena's own store (MSRP unchanged at $439), leaving Amazon's official storefront as the only channel still selling it, near full price, so the $351 comparison point may not hold much longer. Edge wins on JBL audio and mesh polish; 60S wins on unlimited-range Wave and rider count. ASMAX F1s undercuts both on paper-spec at ~$227.

matchups.blurb: Prior Sena flagship, now discounted under the 60S EVO — and as of July 2026 marked out of stock on Sena's own store, still buyable via Amazon's official Sena storefront near full price. Mesh 3.0 + Wave cellular + Harman Kardon, 24-rider mesh.
cardo_advantage: Sound by JBL 40mm vs Harman Kardon (subjective, but Edge audio well-reviewed)
Universal Bluetooth bridging to any brand

matchups.blurb: Value flagship-class: Mesh + BT 5.3 dual-chip, CloudTalk cellular, offline AI voice — flagship spec at half the price.
cardo_advantage: Proven DMC reliability & Western service network
to_test: Long-term QC on a young brand

matchups.blurb: DuoSync dual-mesh (hardware + software), 30-rider claimed. DTC only.
competitor_advantage: Similar price with more claimed riders

battles.title: PACKTALK EDGEPHONES — uncontested niche
battles.exec_summary: Edgephones are over-ear headphones carrying full 2nd-gen Dynamic Mesh — they let a coach, parent, pit-crew member or open-face rider join a Packtalk mesh network without a helmet-mounted unit. No competitor ships a mesh-networked over-ear product: Sena, ASMAX and Reso can only approximate it by strapping a standard intercom to an open-face helmet, and generic Bluetooth headphones have no group mesh at all. This is white space Cardo owns outright.

matchups.blurb: Sena has no mesh over-ear headphone. Closest workaround is clipping a 5S/50S onto an open-face helmet or headband — bulkier, helmet-oriented, not built for off-helmet use.
cardo_advantage: Joins any Packtalk DMC group instantly
competitor_advantage: A spare 5S/50S can be adapted if already owned

matchups.blurb: Consumer BT over-ear/earbuds. Music and calls only — no motorcycle group intercom, no mesh, no rider-to-rider.
cardo_advantage: Integrates with the rider's existing Packtalk network
competitor_advantage: Cheaper if group comms not needed

battles.title: BEYOND GT/GTS vs Sena smart helmets
battles.exec_summary: Cardo's first fully integrated road helmets (GTS $1,299 carbon / GT $949 fiberglass) put DMC mesh — up to 31 riders — plus JBL audio and ANC into a serviceable, wireless-charging shell, and the GTS just picked up a 2026 Red Dot Award: Product Design. But they land into a Sena smart-helmet line that is years deep and far cheaper: the Specter ($649) matches the touring brief with Mesh 3.0 + Wave cellular at half the GTS price, and the Phantom family starts at $549 (quietly raised from $439). Cardo's own July 2026 award announcement also revealed the GTS's MSRP rose from $1,199 to $1,299 and its launch slipped from summer to late 2026 — giving Sena more runway before Cardo ships. Cardo's pitch is premium materials, rider count, upgradeable modules and now a design award; Sena's is proven helmet manufacturing, cellular range and price.

matchups.blurb: Sena's premium modular touring smart helmet. Mesh 3.0 + Wave LTE + BT, 2nd-gen Harman Kardon, 30h talk, Safe Power Mode.
competitor_advantage: Sena's own helmet factory & track record

matchups.blurb: 60S-derived full-face integrated line: base $549 (up from $439), Extreme Bass $599, ANC $649, plus Camera variant. Mesh 3.0 + Harman Kardon + AI noise cancellation.
competitor_advantage: Base Phantom is ~$650 cheaper than GTS
Made in Sena's high-tech helmet factory

matchups.blurb: Modular smart helmet with Sound by Harman Kardon and integrated mesh.
competitor_advantage: Less than half the GTS price

battles.title: VENTURE MX vs Sena Outlander
battles.exec_summary: The Venture is billed as the world's first integrated-mesh motocross helmet, built on the Leatt Moto 8.5 platform with DMC mesh and JBL-partnered audio — a genuine first for the pure off-road segment. Its nearest rival is Sena's Outlander dual-sport smart helmet at $599: $200 cheaper, more road-versatile, and carrying Mesh 3.0 + Wave cellular. Venture owns the MX-specific niche; Outlander wins on price and dual-sport flexibility.

matchups.blurb: Sena's first adventure/dual-sport smart helmet. Mesh 3.0 + Wave LTE + BT, Sound by Harman Kardon, 24-rider mesh.
